chapter_title.en: To the governor of Basra upon learning that he had accepted an invitation to a lavish banquet

parts.text: اعْزُبِي عَنِّي! فَوَاللهِ لَا أَذِلُّ لَكِ فَتَسْتَذِلِّينِي، وَلَا أَسْلَسُ لَكِ فَتَقُودِينِي. وَايْمُ اللهِ ـ يَمِيناً أسْتَثْنِي فِي

text: هَا بِمَشِيئَةِ اللهِ ـ لَأَرُوضَنَّ نَفْسِي رِيَاضَةً تَهِشُّ مَعَهَا إِلَى الْقُرْصِ إِذَا قَدَرْتُ عَلَيْهِ مَطْعُوماً، وَتَقْنَعُ بِالْمِلْحِ مَأْدُوماً، وَلَأَدَعَنَّ مُقْلَتِي كَعَيْنِ مَاءٍ، نَضَبَ مَعِينُهَا مُسْتَفْرِغَةً دُمُوعَهَا.

en.sayed-ali-raza: Get away from me, for, by Allāh, I do not bow before you so that you may humiliate me, nor do I let loose the reins for you so that you may drive me away. I swear by Allāh an oath wherein I, except the will of Allāh, shall so train myself that I will feel joyed if I get one loaf for eating, and be content with only salt to season it. I shall let my eyes empty themselves of tears like the stream whose water has flown away.
